spec_match.rationale: The explorer now uses named-feature(<ident>) syntax in its visible examples and live CSS.supports() checks.

interactive.rationale: The cards are generated from live CSS.supports() probes and now include a registry-boundary check for an unknown named feature.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page still demonstrates the @supports condition family while accurately presenting named-feature() as the new Chrome 150 addition.

edge_cases.rationale: The explorer now includes named-feature(this-is-not-registered), showing that unknown registered-name candidates return false rather than throwing.

references.rationale: The see-also list keeps the ChromeStatus feature link.

design_system.rationale: The touched card layout uses existing design tokens and removes the inline query-result style.

summary: Repaired after chrome-devtools-mcp showed the old named-feature card and code sample used the invalid quoted form. The page now uses identifier syntax and includes an unknown-identifier false case.
